revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hikawa-Nyotai-jinja]][[ja:氷川女体神社]] {{Expand Japanese|氷川女体神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hikawa Nyotai Shrine | native_name = 氷川女体神社 | image = 氷川女体神社－拝殿.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hikawa Nyotai Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.886914|139.693767}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kushinadahime|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|es|Kushinadahime|fr|Kushinada-hime|it|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|mni|ꯀꯨꯁꯤꯅꯗꯍꯤꯃꯦ|ru|Кусинада-химэ|simple|Kushinadahime|sr|Кушинадахиме|zh|櫛名田比賣|qid=Q505469|1=Kushinadahime}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hikawa Nyotai Shrine'''|氷川女体神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya|lt=ichinomiya|ca|Ichinomiya (Xinto)|de|Ichi-no-miya|en|Ichinomiya|fr|Ichi-no-miya|id|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|ko|이치노미야|pt|Ichinomiya (templo)|simple|Ichinomiya|th|อิจิโนมิยะ|uk|Ітіномія (титул)|zh|一宮|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} ** P1001: {{ill|Musashi Province|lt=Musashi Province|ar|محافظة موساشي|ca|Província de Musashi|ckb|پارێزگای موساشی|cs|Provincie Musaši|de|Provinz Musashi|en|Musashi Province|eo|Provinco Musaŝi|es|Provincia de Musashi|fa|ولایت موساشی|fr|Province de Musashi|gl|Provincia de Musashi|hak|Musashi-koet|id|Provinsi Musashi|it|Musashi (provincia)|ja|武蔵国|ko|무사시국|lt|Musašio žemė|nl|Musashi (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Musashi|pt|Província de Musashi|ru|Мусаси (провинция)|simple|Musashi Province|sk|Musaši (provincia)|sr|Провинција Мусаши|th|แคว้นมูซาชิ|tr|Musashi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Мусаші|vi|Musashi (tỉnh)|zh_min_nan|Musasi-kok|zh_yue|武藏國|zh|武藏國|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11546334|ltq=Q11546334|qid=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 15 ** P155: {{ill|Tsuki Shrine|lt=Tsuki Shrine|ja|調神社|qid=Q11631781|1=Tsuki Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039735|ltq=Q135039735|qid=Q135039735|1=Yokomino Shrine (Q135039735)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1130733|ltq=Q1130733|qid=Q1130733|1=Midori-ku, Saitama}} * {{ill|Musashi Province|lt=Musashi Province|ar|محافظة موساشي|ca|Província de Musashi|ckb|پارێزگای موساشی|cs|Provincie Musaši|de|Provinz Musashi|en|Musashi Province|eo|Provinco Musaŝi|es|Provincia de Musashi|fa|ولایت موساشی|fr|Province de Musashi|gl|Provincia de Musashi|hak|Musashi-koet|id|Provinsi Musashi|it|Musashi (provincia)|ja|武蔵国|ko|무사시국|lt|Musašio žemė|nl|Musashi (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Musashi|pt|Província de Musashi|ru|Мусаси (провинция)|simple|Musashi Province|sk|Musaši (provincia)|sr|Провинција Мусаши|th|แคว้นมูซาชิ|tr|Musashi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Мусаші|vi|Musashi (tỉnh)|zh_min_nan|Musasi-kok|zh_yue|武藏國|zh|武藏國|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} * {{ill|Q11637033|ltq=Q11637033|qid=Q11637033|1=Adachi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.886914|139.693767}} == image (P18) == * 氷川女体神社－拝殿.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Kushinadahime|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|es|Kushinadahime|fr|Kushinada-hime|it|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|mni|ꯀꯨꯁꯤꯅꯗꯍꯤꯃꯦ|ru|Кусинада-химэ|simple|Kushinadahime|sr|Кушинадахиме|zh|櫛名田比賣|qid=Q505469|1=Kushinadahime}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181703 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Q11630208|ltq=Q11630208|qid=Q11630208|1=West Canal}} == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 258771689 == postal code (P281) == * 336-0916https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 4752566 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8030005000159 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00645351 == Commons category (P373) == * Hikawa-Nyotai-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Takehimeno Shrine|lt=Takehimeno Shrine|qid=Q135039733|1=Takehimeno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県さいたま市緑区宮本2丁目17-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '埼玉県桶川市篠津', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hikawa-Nyotai-jinja}} [[JA:氷川女体神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11549599}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Hikawa Nyotai Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:氷川女体神社－拝殿.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|qid=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}{{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Saitama|Q1130733|qid=Q1130733|lt=Midori Ward|1=Midori-ku, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Miyamoto, Saitama|Q17215859|qid=Q17215859|lt=Miyamoto|1=Miyamoto}} 2-17-1 |位置 = {{coord|35|53|13|N|139|41|37.6|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|qid=Q505469|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |社格 = {{Ublist| (styled as) {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} | former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system#Miscellaneous shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} }} |創建 = Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Hikawa Nyotai Shrine |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|qid=Q2924|1=October 8}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Hikawa
Nyotai Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hikawa Nyotai Shrine''' (ひかわにょたいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Saitama|Q1130733|qid=Q1130733|lt=Midori Ward|1=Midori-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Its registered {{ill|lt=religious corporation|Q7311343|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} name uses old characters: '''氷川女體神社'''{{Cite web |ja |url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=8030005000159 |title=Information on Hikawa Nyotai Shrine |website=National Tax Agency Corporate Number Publication Site |access-date=2024-01-02}}. It styles itself as a {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. It is a member of the National Ichinomiya Association. Its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system#Miscellaneous shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === {{定義リスト2 | Principal deity | * '''{{ill|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|qid=Q505469|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}''' - wife of {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|qid=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} in Ōmiya ({{ill|lt=Ōmiya-ku, Saitama|Q153629|qid=Q153629|1=Ōmiya-ku, Saitama}}) (principal deity: Susanoo-no-Mikoto) is treated as the “male-body shrine,” while this shrine corresponds to the “female-body shrine”{{Cite book |ja |author=Shōfuku Tankyū Junpai no Kai |title=Complete Guide to Pilgrimage of Ichinomiya Across Japan |edition=Definitive edition |publisher=Meitsu Shuppan |date=2017-12 |isbn=9784780419443 |page=31}}. | Accompanying deities | * {{ill|lt=Mihotsuhime|Q6378698|qid=Q6378698|lt=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} }} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} by inviting the deity from {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|qid=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}}. 'Dai Nihon Chimei Jisho' identifies it as a candidate shrine for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} “{{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|qid=Q11637033|1=Adachi district}} Takehime Shrine” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but the stronger theory is that Takehime Shrine is {{ill|lt=Takehime Shrine|Q65734340|qid=Q65734340|1=Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)}} in Shinotsu, {{ill|lt=Okegawa|Q570333|qid=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|qid=Q6766|1=1591}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} donated 50 koku of shrine land{{Sfn|井上|1998|p=89}}. Early-modern documents and the plaque on the worship hall describe it as “{{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}.” This interprets Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine, this shrine, and {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Saitama)|Q11364245|qid=Q11364245|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine}} (Hioji-sha) as a unified complex; since Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine is Musashi Ichinomiya, this shrine is considered included in that status (see below). Also, this is said to be the birthplace of Ichinomiya pilgrimage, as {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|qid=Q11544184|1=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi}} began his circuit from here in the Edo period. For these reasons, it is now, together with Hikawa Shrine, a member of the National Ichinomiya Association. In the '{{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|qid=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}},' it is listed under the shrine name “Moto-Hikawa Shrine.” {{Clear|right}} === The three Hikawa shrines around Minuma === {| class="wikitable" style="float:right" ! Shrine !! Alias !! Principal deity !! Relationship |- |'''Hikawa Shrine''' | Male-body shrine ||{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}|| Husband |- |'''Hikawa Nyotai Shrine''' | Female-body shrine ||{{ill|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|qid=Q505469|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}|| Wife |- | '''Nakayama Shrine'''
{{Small|(Naka-Hikawa Shrine)}} | Hioji-sha
{{Small|(Hioji-sha)}} ||{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}|| Child (descendant) |} This shrine, Hikawa Shrine, and Nakayama Shrine—located near former {{ill|lt=Minuma|Q6869769|qid=Q6869769|1=Minuma}}—lie on a straight line{{Sfn|青木|1984|p=11}}. The {{ill|lt=sun|Q525|qid=Q525|1=Sun}} sets at summer solstice in the west-northwest at Hikawa Shrine, and rises at winter solstice from the east-southeast at Hikawa Nyotai Shrine, an intentional arrangement to accurately track the {{ill|lt=calendar|Q12132|qid=Q12132|1=Calendar}} important for {{ill|lt=rice cultivation|Q734080|qid=Q734080|1=Rice Cultivation}}. The relationship among the three shrines is as shown in the table at right. From this, one theory holds that the three formed a unified Hikawa shrine complex, with Minuma as a sacred pond “Onuma,” creating a vast sacred domain{{Cite book |ja |author=Ōmiya City |author-link=Ōmiya City |title=Past and Present of Ōmiya |publisher=Ōmiya City |date=1980-11 |id={{JP番号|81007009}} |page=28}}. {{Clear|right}} === Precincts === * Main sanctuary ** In {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|qid=Q7588|1=1667}}), fourth shogun {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ietsuna|Q323422|qid=Q323422|1=Tokugawa Ietsuna}} ordered {{ill|lt=Abe Tadaaki|Q4666449|qid=Q4666449|1=Abe Tadaaki}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Oshi Castle|Q1074393|qid=Q1074393|1=Oshi Castle}}, to rebuild it. The ridge-tag from this reconstruction reads “Musashi Ichinomiya Hikawa Nyotai Daimyōjin,” bearing the Musashi Ichinomiya title. * Iwafune Festival ritual site ruins ** At the end of the Miyuki path in front of the precincts is the “Iwafune Festival Ritual Site Ruins.” Originally, the most important ritual of Hikawa Nyotai Shrine was the Ofune Festival held at {{ill|lt=Minuma|Q6869769|qid=Q6869769|1=Minuma}}. However, in {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 13 (1728), Minuma was reclaimed and the Ofune Festival could no longer be held there. Therefore, in the following year, a ritual site with a round island in a pond was established at what had been part of Minuma, and an “Iwafune Festival” was held in place of the Ofune Festival. The Iwafune Festival ceased by early Meiji, but the ruins remain in good preservation{{Sfn|井上|1998|p=14}}. Hikawa-Nyotai jinja Haiden 202105.jpg|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine, worship hall 氷川女体神社－鳥居.jpg|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine, torii 氷川女体神社 祭祀遺跡.jpg|Iwafune Festival ritual site ruins === Affiliated and minor shrines === Multiple shrines stand behind the main sanctuary. === Cultural properties === === Important Art Object (nationally recognized) === * Mitsurin-mon Hyōgo-kusari tachi, 1 sword - recognized April 27, 1948 (Shōwa 23) === Saitama Prefecture-designated tangible cultural properties === * Mitsurin-mon Hyōgo-kusari tachi, 1 sword (craft) - designated March 28, 1972 (same item as the nationally recognized Important Art Object){{Sfn|浦和市教育委員会|2000|p=5}}{{Cite web |ja |url=https://www.city.saitama.lg.jp/004/005/006/003/p001267_d/fil/bunkazaiitiran.pdf |title=List of designated/registered cultural properties in Saitama City |publisher=Saitama City |date=2024-08-01 |access-date=2024-10-04}} * Hikawa Nyotai Shrine mikoshi, 1 (craft) - designated March 28, 1972{{Sfn|浦和市教育委員会|2000|p=5}} * Pair of peony-pattern heishi bottles, 2 vessels (craft) - designated March 9, 1973. Deposited at {{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|qid=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}}{{Cite book |ja |editor=Urawa City Board of Education |title=Cultural Properties of Urawa |publisher=Urawa City Board of Education |date=1986-10-01 |id={{JP番号|87015667}} |page=51}} * Paper manuscript ink-written Daihannya Haramitta-kyō, 539 scrolls (classics) - designated March 28, 1972 * Hikawa Nyotai Shrine building, 1 structure (architecture) - designated March 16, 2007 === Saitama City-designated cultural properties === {{定義リスト2 | {{underline|Tangible cultural properties}} | * Old shrine treasures of Hikawa Nyotai Shrine, one set (craft) - designated May 21, 1969{{Sfn|浦和市教育委員会|2000|p=22}} * Shinmei-gū plaque, calligraphy by monk Kōjun, 1 panel (calligraphy) - designated March 28, 1988{{Sfn|浦和市教育委員会|2000|p=25}} * Hōjō Ujitsuna regulation notice 1 document, Hōjō clan seal letter 1 document (old documents) - designated March 31, 1961{{Sfn|浦和市教育委員会|2000|p=27}} * Hikawa Nyotai Shrine land donation and vermilion-seal documents, 12 documents (old documents) - designated March 29, 1979{{Sfn|浦和市教育委員会|2000|p=31}} | {{underline|Intangible folk cultural property}} | * Nagoshi Harae at Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - designated March 28, 1985 | {{underline|Historic site}} | * Hikawa Nyotai Shrine Iwafune Festival ritual site ruins - designated March 29, 1979 | {{underline|Natural monument}} | * Shrine grove of Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - designated July 1, 1965 }} === Local information === {{定義リスト2 | Location | * {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|qid=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}{{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Saitama|Q1130733|qid=Q1130733|lt=Midori Ward|1=Midori-ku, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Miyamoto, Saitama|Q17215859|qid=Q17215859|lt=Miyamoto|1=Miyamoto}} 2-17-1 ** The place name around this shrine is called Mimuro; “Mimuro no Hikawa Shrine” refers to this shrine. | Access | * From {{ill|lt=Higashi-Urawa Station|Q5753897|qid=Q5753897|1=Higashi-Urawa Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|qid=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Musashino Line|Q1191671|qid=Q1191671|1=Musashino Line}}), by {{ill|lt=Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|Q11422566|qid=Q11422566|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus}} ** Get off at “Asahizakaue” bus stop (Ura04-3), about 5 minutes on foot ** Get off at “Shibahara Elementary School” bus stop (Ura04-2 or Ura04-3), about 10 minutes on foot }} === Concurrently administered shrines === * {{ill|lt=Motobuto Hikawa Shrine|Q22120087|qid=Q22120087|lt=Motobuto Hikawa Shrine|1=Hikawa Shrine (Urawa-ku)}} - primary administrative shrine * {{ill|lt=Motomachi Yakumo Shrine|Q0|qid=Q0|lt=Motomachi Yakumo Shrine|1=Motomachi Yakumo Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mikado Inari Shrine|Q0|qid=Q0|lt=Mikado Inari Shrine|1=Mikado Inari Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book |ja |author=Aoki Yoshinobu |title=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine |publisher=Sakitama Shuppankai |series=Sakitama Bunko 1 |date=1984-01-01 |id={{NDLSearch|R100000001-I11121123434278}} |ref={{SfnRef|青木|1984}}}} * {{Cite book |ja |author=Inoue Kaora |title=Mimuro Monogatari: Centered on Minuma and Hikawa Nyotaisha |publisher=Sakitama Shuppankai |date=1998-04 |isbn=4-87891-112-3 |ref={{SfnRef|井上|1998}}}} * {{Cite book |ja |editor=Urawa City Board of Education |title=Comprehensive Catalog of Designated Cultural Properties |volume=As of April 1, 2000 |publisher=Urawa City Board of Education |date=2000-10 |id={{JP番号|20114784}} |ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{Cite book |ja |author=Aoki Yoshinobu |title=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine |publisher=Urawa City Local Culture Association |series=Urawa
